commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can sanitize the new diagnostic and add one regression without requiring product judgment.; Needs attention: The diff adds a concrete terminal/log injection risk by interpolating an unsanitized CLI requestId into a device-approval error message.; Review finding: [P1] Sanitize the stale requestId before rendering it — src/cli/devices-cli.runtime.ts:196 (sha=50b3b17784a52c6a22e06fb76e95ea3333408815)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the stale local `openclaw devices approve` fallback’s silent null result with an actionable error that counts other pending requests and routes operators to `openclaw devices list`, with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +93. Total +142 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main returns null from the local approve fallback when the supplied id is missing and no gateway requestId is present, and the caller then prints `unknown requestId`. The PR body also includes a real ECS before case and local gateway after case, but I did not run a live reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can sanitize the new diagnostic and add one regression without requiring product judgment.
